title: Prevent server-side request forgery
description: Server-side requests that use unvalidated user input can be exploited for server-side request forgery (SSRF) attacks. Attackers can force the server to make requests to internal systems, cloud metadata services, or other unauthorized targets.
recommendation: Validate and whitelist URLs, implement request filtering, and restrict access to internal networks.

noncompliant.description: Using unvalidated user input in server-side requests allows attackers to access internal systems, cloud metadata, or scan networks.

compliant.description: Validating URLs against whitelists, blocking private IP ranges, and using restricted network dialers prevents SSRF attacks.
